frameworks.name: motion-form integration
frameworks.one_line: motion and orientation signals interact within motion-integrating receptive fields

frameworks.name: global optic flow processing
frameworks.one_line: extends motion-form interaction to the higher stage where optic flow is extracted

frameworks.name: motion aftereffect adaptation
frameworks.one_line: MAE duration used as a psychophysical probe of motion-processing stage

core_question: Do motion-form interactions occur beyond motion integration, at the level where optic flow components are extracted?
key_assumptions: MAE duration indexes the adapted motion-processing stage
phantom MAE isolates global motion / optic flow processing
radial vs concentric pedestals selectively probe parallel vs orthogonal orientation relative to flow
manipulations: radial (parallel) vs concentric (orthogonal) counterphase pedestal gratings
phantom MAE: orientation signals in adapting vs nonadapting sectors
key_findings: motion adaptation was suppressed most by orientation signals orthogonal to optic flow direction
the suppression pattern was obtained also for the phantom MAE across separate visual-field sectors
interpretations: motion-form interactions take place at the global motion level where optic flow is extracted, not only at motion integration
